articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' Economic Roadmap, Focus on Green Growth and Digital Transformation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'Vision Bharat 2047', a comprehensive economic roadmap aimed at transforming India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The plan emphasizes sustainable development, green energy transition, and robust digital infrastructure as key pillars for growth.
• It outlines strategic investments in renewable energy, electric mobility, and circular economy principles to achieve environmental sustainability.
• Significant focus is placed on enhancing digital public infrastructure, fostering innovation, and upskilling the workforce for the future economy.
• The roadmap also targets boosting manufacturing capabilities and integrating India further into global value chains.

articles.imageDescription: Infographic depicting 'Vision Bharat 2047' with symbols of green energy, digital connectivity, and economic growth.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070, a target that aligns closely with the green growth objectives of Vision Bharat 2047.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy Rights in Data Protection Act Challenge, Sets New Precedent for Digital Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, but emphasized the need for strict adherence to privacy safeguards.
• The ruling clarified that while the state has legitimate interests in data processing, these must be proportionate and respect the fundamental right to privacy.
• The Court directed the government to establish an independent Data Protection Board with robust powers to ensure accountability and grievance redressal.
• Concerns raised regarding potential for state surveillance and data misuse were addressed, with the Court stressing judicial oversight and transparent operating procedures.
• This judgment is expected to significantly influence digital governance frameworks and corporate data handling practices in India.

Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court ruled on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.

Directed the establishment of an independent Data Protection Board.

mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Supreme Court's judgment on the DPDP Act in balancing state interests with individual privacy rights.
Discuss the implications of this ruling for digital governance, corporate data practices, and the future of fundamental rights in the digital age.
Examine the challenges in effectively implementing the DPDP Act and ensuring the independence and effectiveness of the Data Protection Board.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment, with digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, laying the groundwork for data protection laws.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting, Pushes for Global Debt Restructuring and Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India today hosted the second G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, focusing on pressing global economic challenges.
• A key agenda item was the urgent need for comprehensive global debt restructuring mechanisms, particularly for low-income countries facing mounting debt burdens.
• India strongly advocated for enhanced climate finance commitments from developed nations and innovative financing solutions for green transitions in developing economies.
• Discussions also covered strengthening multilateral development banks, addressing supply chain disruptions, and ensuring global food and energy security.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on coordinated policy responses to foster stable and inclusive global economic growth.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) for Future Manned Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable launch system.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling multiple uses of rocket components, making space missions more economical.
• This achievement is a major milestone for India's ambitious Gaganyaan manned space mission and future space exploration endeavors.
• The RLV program is expected to bolster India's position in the global commercial space launch market.

articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD is designed to act as a flying testbed to evaluate various technologies, including hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and powered cruise flight.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns, Signals Cautious Approach to Monetary Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes amidst persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• While acknowledging robust domestic growth, the MPC highlighted the need for a cautious approach to ensure inflation aligns with the target band of 4% (+/- 2%).
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to keep inflation expectations anchored.
• Experts anticipate the RBI will continue to monitor evolving economic indicators before considering any future rate adjustments.

articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic trends and a magnifying glass over inflation data.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

articles.headline: New National Mission for Himalayan Ecosystems Launched, Emphasizing Sustainable Tourism and Biodiversity Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a revamped National Mission for Sustaining the Himalayan Ecosystem (NMSHE).
• The mission now includes a stronger focus on promoting sustainable tourism practices and enhancing biodiversity conservation efforts across the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• Key objectives include climate change adaptation strategies, capacity building for local communities, and scientific research on glacier health and water resources.
• The initiative aims to protect the fragile Himalayan environment while supporting the livelihoods of mountain communities.
• It integrates indigenous knowledge with modern scientific approaches for holistic ecosystem management.

articles.headline: Border Infrastructure Push Continues in Eastern Sector, Enhancing Connectivity and Strategic Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Defence today announced accelerated progress on border infrastructure development projects in the Eastern Sector, particularly along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The projects include the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels, aimed at improving military mobility and civilian connectivity in remote areas.
• This push is part of a broader strategy to enhance India's strategic preparedness and ensure rapid deployment capabilities in challenging terrains.
• The development also seeks to integrate border villages into the national mainstream by improving access to markets and essential services.
• Modern construction techniques and indigenous materials are being utilized to overcome geographical challenges and ensure project completion within stipulated timelines.

articles.headline: Government Expands Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, Aims for Universal Health Records by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry today announced a significant expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), setting an ambitious target for universal digital health records by 2030.
• The expanded mission will focus on integrating more private healthcare providers and pharmacies into the digital ecosystem, alongside public facilities.
• Key features include enhanced interoperability of health data, robust data privacy safeguards, and a user-friendly interface for citizens to access their health information.
• The initiative aims to streamline healthcare delivery, reduce administrative burdens, and improve health outcomes through data-driven insights.
• Pilot projects in remote and tribal areas will be scaled up to ensure equitable access to digital health services across the country.

articles.headline: India-Japan Sign Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) Expansion, Boosting Bilateral Trade
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan today signed an expansion of their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA), aiming to further boost bilateral trade and investment.
• The revised agreement includes new provisions for cooperation in emerging technologies, digital trade, and resilient supply chains.
• Both nations committed to reducing non-tariff barriers and streamlining customs procedures to facilitate smoother trade flows.
• The expansion is expected to benefit sectors like automotive, electronics, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy, fostering greater collaboration.
• This move underscores the deepening strategic and economic partnership between India and Japan, crucial for Indo-Pacific stability and growth.

articles.headline: State Elections: High Voter Turnout Reported in Phase II Polling, Focus on Regional Issues and Development
articles.explainer:
• Phase II of the ongoing state assembly elections today witnessed a high voter turnout across several constituencies, indicating strong public engagement.
• Voters expressed concerns primarily around local governance, infrastructure development, employment opportunities, and agricultural policies.
• Political analysts noted a heightened focus on regional issues and the performance of incumbent state governments, rather than national narratives.
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) reported smooth conduct of polling, with robust security arrangements and strict adherence to electoral guidelines.
• The outcome of these elections is anticipated to reflect evolving political dynamics and public priorities at the state level.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Growing Water Scarcity in Peninsular India, Calls for Integrated Water Management Strategies
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the Central Water Commission today warned of escalating water scarcity in Peninsular India, exacerbated by erratic monsoons and over-extraction of groundwater.
• The report identifies several states facing critical water stress, impacting agricultural productivity, urban water supply, and industrial operations.
• It calls for urgent implementation of integrated water resource management (IWRM) strategies, including rainwater harvesting, efficient irrigation, and wastewater treatment.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and inter-state cooperation for effective river basin management.
• Climate change impacts, such as altered rainfall patterns and increased evaporation rates, are cited as major contributors to the growing crisis.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in AI-Powered Disaster Prediction Systems Showcases Potential for Early Warning
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers today announced a significant breakthrough in developing AI-powered disaster prediction systems, capable of providing more accurate and timely early warnings.
• The new models integrate satellite imagery, meteorological data, and historical disaster records using deep learning algorithms to predict events like floods, droughts, and cyclones.
• Pilot projects demonstrated improved accuracy in forecasting the intensity and trajectory of natural hazards, allowing for better preparedness and response.
• This technology has the potential to save lives and minimize economic losses by enabling proactive disaster management strategies.
• Further development will focus on scaling these systems for national deployment and integrating them with existing disaster management frameworks.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Batteries Attracts Major Investment, Bolstering India's EV Manufacturing Ambitions
articles.explainer:

• This surge in investment is expected to bolster India's manufacturing capabilities for electric vehicle (EV) batteries and energy storage solutions.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependence on critical battery components and establish a robust domestic supply chain.
• Government officials highlighted the potential for job creation, technology transfer, and achieving self-reliance in the rapidly growing EV sector.
• The move aligns with India's broader climate goals and its commitment to transitioning towards cleaner transportation and energy systems.

articles.headline: India Engages with Quad Partners on Maritime Security and Indo-Pacific Stability Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India today participated in a high-level virtual meeting with its Quad partners – Australia, Japan, and the United States – to discuss enhanced cooperation on maritime security and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Key discussions focused on strengthening maritime domain awareness, combating illegal fishing, and ensuring freedom of navigation in international waters.
• The partners reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law and promoting peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Initiatives for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) in the region were also reviewed, emphasizing coordinated responses to emergencies.
• This engagement underscores the Quad's growing role as a crucial platform for addressing geopolitical and security challenges in the vital Indo-Pacific region.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Shows Progress in Skill Development and Digital Learning
articles.explainer:

• The report noted enhanced focus on skill development and vocational training at various educational levels, aligning with industry demands.
• Digital learning initiatives, including online courses and educational technology platforms, have seen widespread adoption, especially in higher education.
• Challenges remain in teacher training, infrastructure development in remote areas, and ensuring equitable access to quality education for all.
• The government reiterated its commitment to the NEP's long-term vision of transforming India into a knowledge society and global powerhouse.

articles.headline: Commemoration of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's Legacy: Discussions on Social Justice and Constitutional Values
articles.explainer:
• Following Ambedkar Jayanti (April 14), numerous seminars and discussions continued today across the nation, commemorating the enduring legacy of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.
• Scholars and policymakers deliberated on Ambedkar's vision for social justice, equality, and the eradication of caste-based discrimination in contemporary India.
• Emphasis was placed on the relevance of his constitutional philosophy, particularly the principles of liberty, equality, fraternity, and justice.
• Discussions also highlighted the need to uphold secular values and protect the rights of marginalized communities, reflecting Ambedkar's lifelong struggle.
• The events served as a reminder of the ongoing efforts required to build an inclusive and equitable society as envisioned by the architect of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Status Sought for Traditional Indian Textile Weaving Techniques
articles.explainer:
• India has submitted a fresh nomination to UNESCO seeking Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) status for a cluster of traditional Indian textile weaving techniques.
• The nomination highlights the intricate craftsmanship, diverse regional variations, and the socio-economic significance of these weaving traditions.
• It aims to protect and promote the rich heritage of Indian textiles, ensure the livelihoods of artisan communities, and raise global awareness.
• Efforts include documentation, capacity building for weavers, and creation of market linkages to sustain these invaluable cultural practices.
• Recognition by UNESCO is expected to provide a significant boost to the preservation and international visibility of India's textile heritage.

articles.headline: Indian Railways Launches High-Speed Freight Corridor, Revolutionizing Logistics and Supply Chains
articles.explainer:
• Indian Railways today inaugurated a new segment of the dedicated high-speed freight corridor, marking a significant leap in modernizing India's logistics infrastructure.
• The corridor is designed to allow freight trains to operate at much higher speeds and carry heavier loads, drastically reducing transit times for goods.
• This initiative is expected to boost industrial growth by improving supply chain efficiency, reducing transportation costs, and enhancing competitiveness for businesses.
• It will also help decongest existing passenger rail lines, allowing for improved punctuality and expansion of passenger services.
• The project utilizes advanced signaling systems and electric traction, aligning with India's goals for sustainable and efficient transportation.

articles.headline: New Solar Energy Policy Incentivizes Rooftop Installations and Decentralized Power Generation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today announced a new solar energy policy designed to significantly boost rooftop solar installations and decentralized power generation across India.
• The policy introduces enhanced financial incentives, including higher subsidies and easier loan access, for residential and commercial rooftop solar projects.
• It streamlines the approval process and promotes net metering policies to allow consumers to feed surplus electricity back into the grid.
• The initiative aims to reduce reliance on conventional power sources, lower electricity bills for consumers, and contribute to India's renewable energy targets.
• This focus on decentralized generation empowers local communities and reduces transmission losses, fostering a more resilient energy ecosystem.

articles.headline: National Nutrition Mission Emphasizes Maternal and Child Health in New Phase
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development today launched a new phase of the National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan), with an enhanced focus on maternal and child health.
• The revamped mission prioritizes interventions for reducing anemia among women and children, improving breastfeeding practices, and addressing severe acute malnutrition.
• It integrates technology-driven monitoring systems to track nutritional outcomes and ensure targeted delivery of services in vulnerable regions.
• Community-level awareness campaigns and capacity building for frontline health workers are key components to foster behavioral change.
• The initiative aims to achieve significant reductions in malnutrition and improve health indicators for the most susceptible segments of the population.

highlights: Government unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' economic roadmap focusing on green growth and digital transformation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'Vision Bharat 2047':
1. It aims to transform India into a developed nation by 2047.
2. It primarily focuses on agricultural growth and rural infrastructure.
3. Green energy transition and digital infrastructure are identified as key pillars.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as 'Vision Bharat 2047' aims for India to be a developed nation by its centenary of independence. Statement 2 is incorrect as the primary focus is on green growth, digital transformation, and global competitiveness, not solely agriculture and rural infrastructure. Statement 3 is correct as green energy transition and digital infrastructure are explicitly mentioned as key pillars.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is mandated by the Supreme Court to be established as an independent entity to ensure accountability and grievance redressal under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act specifically directed the government to establish an independent Data Protection Board with robust powers to ensure accountability and grievance redressal, as mentioned in the article.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's role in the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, which of the following were key agenda items advocated by India?
1. Global debt restructuring for low-income countries.
2. Enhanced climate finance commitments from developed nations.
3. Strengthening multilateral development banks.
4. Promoting bilateral trade agreements exclusively with G7 nations.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: India strongly advocated for global debt restructuring, enhanced climate finance commitments, and strengthening multilateral development banks. Promoting bilateral trade agreements exclusively with G7 nations is not a stated agenda of India at the G20 FMCBG meeting; G20 focuses on broader multilateral economic cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question: The Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) is an ambitious project of which Indian organization?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) is a project of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) aimed at developing technologies for a fully reusable launch system to reduce the cost of space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Mission for Sustaining the Himalayan Ecosystem (NMSHE):
1. It is one of the eight missions under India's National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC).
2. The revamped mission places a stronger focus on promoting sustainable tourism and biodiversity conservation.
3. It primarily focuses on developing hydroelectric power projects in the Himalayan region.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as NMSHE is indeed one of the eight missions under NAPCC. Statement 2 is correct as the revamped mission emphasizes sustainable tourism and biodiversity conservation. Statement 3 is incorrect; while hydropower is relevant to the Himalayas, the primary focus of NMSHE is on ecosystem sustenance, climate change adaptation, and biodiversity, not primarily hydropower development.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights Growing Water Scarcity in Peninsular India, Calls for Integrated Water Management Strategies
GS1.explainer:
• A new report released by the Central Water Commission today warned of escalating water scarcity in Peninsular India, exacerbated by erratic monsoons and over-extraction of groundwater.
• The report identifies several states facing critical water stress, impacting agricultural productivity, urban water supply, and industrial operations.
• It calls for urgent implementation of integrated water resource management (IWRM) strategies, including rainwater harvesting, efficient irrigation, and wastewater treatment.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and inter-state cooperation for effective river basin management.
• Climate change impacts, such as altered rainfall patterns and increased evaporation rates, are cited as major contributors to the growing crisis.

GS1.headline: Commemoration of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's Legacy: Discussions on Social Justice and Constitutional Values
GS1.explainer:
• Following Ambedkar Jayanti (April 14), numerous seminars and discussions continued today across the nation, commemorating the enduring legacy of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.
• Scholars and policymakers deliberated on Ambedkar's vision for social justice, equality, and the eradication of caste-based discrimination in contemporary India.
• Emphasis was placed on the relevance of his constitutional philosophy, particularly the principles of liberty, equality, fraternity, and justice.
• Discussions also highlighted the need to uphold secular values and protect the rights of marginalized communities, reflecting Ambedkar's lifelong struggle.
• The events served as a reminder of the ongoing efforts required to build an inclusive and equitable society as envisioned by the architect of the Indian Constitution.

• It aims to protect and promote the rich heritage of Indian textiles, ensure the livelihoods of artisan communities, and raise global awareness.
• Efforts include documentation, capacity building for weavers, and creation of market linkages to sustain these invaluable cultural practices.
• Recognition by UNESCO is expected to provide a significant boost to the preservation and international visibility of India's textile heritage.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy Rights in Data Protection Act Challenge, Sets New Precedent for Digital Governance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, but emphasized the need for strict adherence to privacy safeguards.
• The ruling clarified that while the state has legitimate interests in data processing, these must be proportionate and respect the fundamental right to privacy.
• The Court directed the government to establish an independent Data Protection Board with robust powers to ensure accountability and grievance redressal.
• Concerns raised regarding potential for state surveillance and data misuse were addressed, with the Court stressing judicial oversight and transparent operating procedures.
• This judgment is expected to significantly influence digital governance frameworks and corporate data handling practices in India.

• A key agenda item was the urgent need for comprehensive global debt restructuring mechanisms, particularly for low-income countries facing mounting debt burdens.
• India strongly advocated for enhanced climate finance commitments from developed nations and innovative financing solutions for green transitions in developing economies.
• Discussions also covered strengthening multilateral development banks, addressing supply chain disruptions, and ensuring global food and energy security.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on coordinated policy responses to foster stable and inclusive global economic growth.

• Key features include enhanced interoperability of health data, robust data privacy safeguards, and a user-friendly interface for citizens to access their health information.
• The initiative aims to streamline healthcare delivery, reduce administrative burdens, and improve health outcomes through data-driven insights.
• Pilot projects in remote and tribal areas will be scaled up to ensure equitable access to digital health services across the country.

• The revised agreement includes new provisions for cooperation in emerging technologies, digital trade, and resilient supply chains.
• Both nations committed to reducing non-tariff barriers and streamlining customs procedures to facilitate smoother trade flows.
• The expansion is expected to benefit sectors like automotive, electronics, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy, fostering greater collaboration.

• Political analysts noted a heightened focus on regional issues and the performance of incumbent state governments, rather than national narratives.
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) reported smooth conduct of polling, with robust security arrangements and strict adherence to electoral guidelines.

• The partners reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law and promoting peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Initiatives for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) in the region were also reviewed, emphasizing coordinated responses to emergencies.
• This engagement underscores the Quad's growing role as a crucial platform for addressing geopolitical and security challenges in the vital Indo-Pacific region.

GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after those in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: National Nutrition Mission Emphasizes Maternal and Child Health in New Phase
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development today launched a new phase of the National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan), with an enhanced focus on maternal and child health.
• The revamped mission prioritizes interventions for reducing anemia among women and children, improving breastfeeding practices, and addressing severe acute malnutrition.
• It integrates technology-driven monitoring systems to track nutritional outcomes and ensure targeted delivery of services in vulnerable regions.
• Community-level awareness campaigns and capacity building for frontline health workers are key components to foster behavioral change.
• The initiative aims to achieve significant reductions in malnutrition and improve health indicators for the most susceptible segments of the population.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' Economic Roadmap, Focus on Green Growth and Digital Transformation
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'Vision Bharat 2047', a comprehensive economic roadmap aimed at transforming India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The plan emphasizes sustainable development, green energy transition, and robust digital infrastructure as key pillars for growth.
• It outlines strategic investments in renewable energy, electric mobility, and circular economy principles to achieve environmental sustainability.
• Significant focus is placed on enhancing digital public infrastructure, fostering innovation, and upskilling the workforce for the future economy.
• The roadmap also targets boosting manufacturing capabilities and integrating India further into global value chains.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) for Future Manned Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable launch system.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling multiple uses of rocket components, making space missions more economical.
• This achievement is a major milestone for India's ambitious Gaganyaan manned space mission and future space exploration endeavors.
• The RLV program is expected to bolster India's position in the global commercial space launch market.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns, Signals Cautious Approach to Monetary Policy
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes amidst persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• While acknowledging robust domestic growth, the MPC highlighted the need for a cautious approach to ensure inflation aligns with the target band of 4% (+/- 2%).
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to keep inflation expectations anchored.
• Experts anticipate the RBI will continue to monitor evolving economic indicators before considering any future rate adjustments.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

GS3.headline: New National Mission for Himalayan Ecosystems Launched, Emphasizing Sustainable Tourism and Biodiversity Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a revamped National Mission for Sustaining the Himalayan Ecosystem (NMSHE).
• The mission now includes a stronger focus on promoting sustainable tourism practices and enhancing biodiversity conservation efforts across the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• Key objectives include climate change adaptation strategies, capacity building for local communities, and scientific research on glacier health and water resources.
• The initiative aims to protect the fragile Himalayan environment while supporting the livelihoods of mountain communities.
• It integrates indigenous knowledge with modern scientific approaches for holistic ecosystem management.

GS3.headline: Border Infrastructure Push Continues in Eastern Sector, Enhancing Connectivity and Strategic Preparedness
GS3.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Defence today announced accelerated progress on border infrastructure development projects in the Eastern Sector, particularly along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The projects include the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels, aimed at improving military mobility and civilian connectivity in remote areas.
• This push is part of a broader strategy to enhance India's strategic preparedness and ensure rapid deployment capabilities in challenging terrains.
• The development also seeks to integrate border villages into the national mainstream by improving access to markets and essential services.
• Modern construction techniques and indigenous materials are being utilized to overcome geographical challenges and ensure project completion within stipulated timelines.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in AI-Powered Disaster Prediction Systems Showcases Potential for Early Warning
GS3.explainer:
• Indian researchers today announced a significant breakthrough in developing AI-powered disaster prediction systems, capable of providing more accurate and timely early warnings.
• The new models integrate satellite imagery, meteorological data, and historical disaster records using deep learning algorithms to predict events like floods, droughts, and cyclones.
• Pilot projects demonstrated improved accuracy in forecasting the intensity and trajectory of natural hazards, allowing for better preparedness and response.
• This technology has the potential to save lives and minimize economic losses by enabling proactive disaster management strategies.
• Further development will focus on scaling these systems for national deployment and integrating them with existing disaster management frameworks.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Batteries Attracts Major Investment, Bolstering India's EV Manufacturing Ambitions
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage today witnessed significant investment commitments from both domestic and international players.
• This surge in investment is expected to bolster India's manufacturing capabilities for electric vehicle (EV) batteries and energy storage solutions.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependence on critical battery components and establish a robust domestic supply chain.
• Government officials highlighted the potential for job creation, technology transfer, and achieving self-reliance in the rapidly growing EV sector.
• The move aligns with India's broader climate goals and its commitment to transitioning towards cleaner transportation and energy systems.

GS3.headline: New Geospatial Policy to Aid Urban Planning and Resource Mapping for Sustainable Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology today unveiled a new National Geospatial Policy, aimed at leveraging geospatial data and technology for accelerated national development.
• The policy liberalizes access to geospatial data and encourages private sector participation in mapping and data analytics.
• It is expected to significantly aid urban planning, infrastructure development, disaster management, and precise resource mapping for sectors like agriculture and mining.
• The framework emphasizes the creation of a robust national geospatial infrastructure, ensuring data standardization and interoperability.
• This move is anticipated to unlock economic potential, foster innovation, and support sustainable development goals across various domains.

GS3.headline: Indian Railways Launches High-Speed Freight Corridor, Revolutionizing Logistics and Supply Chains
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways today inaugurated a new segment of the dedicated high-speed freight corridor, marking a significant leap in modernizing India's logistics infrastructure.
• The corridor is designed to allow freight trains to operate at much higher speeds and carry heavier loads, drastically reducing transit times for goods.
• This initiative is expected to boost industrial growth by improving supply chain efficiency, reducing transportation costs, and enhancing competitiveness for businesses.
• It will also help decongest existing passenger rail lines, allowing for improved punctuality and expansion of passenger services.
• The project utilizes advanced signaling systems and electric traction, aligning with India's goals for sustainable and efficient transportation.

GS3.headline: New Solar Energy Policy Incentivizes Rooftop Installations and Decentralized Power Generation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today announced a new solar energy policy designed to significantly boost rooftop solar installations and decentralized power generation across India.
• The policy introduces enhanced financial incentives, including higher subsidies and easier loan access, for residential and commercial rooftop solar projects.
• It streamlines the approval process and promotes net metering policies to allow consumers to feed surplus electricity back into the grid.
• The initiative aims to reduce reliance on conventional power sources, lower electricity bills for consumers, and contribute to India's renewable energy targets.
• This focus on decentralized generation empowers local communities and reduces transmission losses, fostering a more resilient energy ecosystem.
